assertions.label: allowable flexural tension Ft (Type S hollow)

raw.title: Unreinforced CMU Bearing Wall — ASD
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Unreinforced CMU Bearing Wall — ASD Unreinforced CMU Bearing Wall — ASD 4″ & 6″ Nominal CMU · Combined Axial + Wind Flexure · Allowable Stress Design per TMS 402-22 TMS 402-22 Chapter 8 §8.2.4 Allowable Axial §8.2.5 Flexural Tension §8.2.6 Combined Loading MDG-2022 WALL SCHEMATIC w = 25.0 psf P = 1.0 k/ft δ h = 9.0' t = 5.625" Top support Btm. support Elevation hollow core t = 5.625" 12" (1 ft) tfs = 1.00" 6" nom. CMU (hollow) Cross Section (per ft) ■ Wall Type & Material TMS 402-22 §8.2 Nominal Wall Thickness 4″ Nominal CMU (actual 3.625″) 6″ Nominal CMU (actual 5.625″) Actual t = 5.625″ · An = 24 in²/ft · S = 46.3 in³/ft · r = 1.624″ Grout Condition Hollow (Ungrouted) Fully Grouted Face-shell bedding for hollow; gross section for grouted Specified Masonry Strength f′m (psi) Typical: 1500–2000 psi for CMU Mortar Type Type S Type N Affects Ft per TMS 402-22 Table 8.2.5.2 ■ Geometry & Loading ASD Load Combination Wall Height h (ft) Clear height between supports Max ASD Vertical Load P (plf) Total axial load at critical section (lb per ft of wall). For tension check, re-run with min P. Interior Wind Pressure w (psf) Lateral pressure on wall face Support Conditions Simply Supported — M = wh²/8 Fixed-Pinned — M = wh²/10 Fixed-Fixed — M = wh²/12 Most conservative and common: simply supported Apply ⅓ stress increase for wind load combination (TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.1) — multiply allowable stresses by 4/3 ▶ Run All Checks ✓ ALL CHECKS PASS Governing D/C = 0.96 · 6″ CMU — Hollow (Ungrouted) · h = 9.0 ft · fʼm = 1500.0 psi · P = 1000.0 plf · w = 25.0 psf SECTION PROPERTIES — PER FOOT OF WALL Property Symbol Value Notes Nominal thickness tnom 6″ Actual thickness t 5.6 in CMU modular dimension Net area An 24.0 in²/ft Face-shell bedding: 2 × t₟s × 12 Net section modulus Sn 46.3 in³/ft Iₙ/(t/2) Net moment of inertia In 130.4 in⁴/ft t³ − (t−2t₟s)³ Radius of gyration r 1.6 in t/√12 (simplified per TMS) Face shell thickness tfs 1.0 in Min. per TMS 602-22 Wall weight wwall 38.0 psf Approximate h/r slenderness h/r 66.5 Short: use Eq. (8-16) h/t slenderness h/t 19.2 OK ≤ 20 KEY DEMANDS & ALLOWABLES 41.7 psi Actual Axial Stress fₐ = P/Aₙ 290.4 psi Allow. Axial Stress Fₐ (eff.) 253.1 ft‑lb/ft Wind Moment at Mid-Height Mₘₐˣ 65.6 psi Actual Flexural Stress fᵇ 495.0 psi Allow. Flexural Stress Fᵇ (eff.) 0.28 Combined Unity fₐ/Fₐ + fᵇ/Fᵇ 23.9 psi Net Flexural Tension fᵇ − fₐ 25.0 psi Allow. Flex. Tension Fₜ (eff.) CODE CHECKS Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Slenderness — h/t Max h/t = 20 for unreinforced bearing walls TMS 402-22 §5.5.1.1 h/t = 19.2 Limit = 20 0.96 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §5.5.1.1 — Slenderness (h/t) Limit: h/t ≤ 20 for unreinforced bearing walls h = 9.0 ft = 108.0 in t = 5.6 in (actual) h/t = 108.0 / 5.6 = 19.2 D/C = 19.2 20.0 = 0.96 Axial Stress fa vs Fa h/r = 66.5 TMS 402-22 §8.2.4.3 fa = 41.7 psi Fa = 290.4 psi 0.14 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.4.3 — Allowable Axial Compressive Stress Eq. (8-16): F a = 0.25 fʼm × [1 − h 140r ²] when h/r ≤ 99 h/r = 108.0/1.6 = 66.5 ≤ 99 ✓ = 0.25 × 1500.0 × [1 − (108.0/(140 × 1.6)²] = 290.4 psi (base) × k = 1.00 → 290.4 psi (eff.) fa = P/An = 1000.0 / 24.0 = 41.7 psi D/C = 41.7 290.4 = 0.14 Flexural Stress fb vs Fb M = 253.1 ft-lb/ft (w = 25.0 psf, h = 9.0 ft) TMS 402-22 §8.2.3 fb = 65.6 psi Fb = 495.0 psi 0.13 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.3 — Allowable Flexural Compressive Stress Support condition: Simply Supported — M max = w·h²/8 Mmax = w × h²/8 = 25.0 × 9.0²/8 = 253.1 ft‑lb/ft = 253.1 × 12 = 3037.5 in‑lb/ft fb = M/S n = 3037.5 / 46.3 = 65.6 psi Fb = 0.33 × fʼm = 0.33 × 1500.0 = 495.0 psi (base) × 1.00 = 495.0 psi (eff.) D/C = 65.6 495.0 = 0.13 Combined Compression — Unity Check fₐ/Fₐ = 0.143 + fᵇ/Fᵇ = 0.133 TMS 402-22 §8.2.6.2 fa Fa + fb Fb ≤ 1.0 0.28 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.6.2 — Combined Compressive Stresses Eq. (8-20): fa Fa + fb Fb ≤ 1.0 = 41.7 290.4 + 65.6 495.0 = 0.143 + 0.133 = 0.276 ≤ 1.0 ✓ D/C (Unity) = 0.276 Net Flexural Tension (fb − fa) vs Ft Fₜ = 25 psi base × 1.00 TMS 402-22 §8.2.5 fnet = 23.9 psi Ft = 25.0 psi 0.96 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.2.5 / §8.2.6.3 — Net Flexural Tension Net tensile stress: f net = f b − f a = 65.6 − 41.7 = 23.9 psi Ft = 25 psi (base, TMS 402-22 Table 8.2.5.2) × 1.00 = 25.0 psi Mortar: Type S · Wall: Hollow/Ungrouted D/C = 23.9 25.0 = 0.96 Engineering Notes & Assumptions: All calculations per TMS 402-22 (Building Code Requirements and Specification for Masonry Structures) and MDG-2022. Section properties use r = t/√12 (simplified, slightly conservative) per standard ASD masonry practice. Hollow (ungrouted) area based on face-shell bedding only: Aₙ = 2 × t₟s × 12 in²/ft. Net I and S based on hollow box section. P is the total ASD vertical load (dead + applicable live) at the critical section. Run a separate check with minimum P (0.6D only) to evaluate the tension case under wind. No wind 1/3 stress increase applied. Check TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.1 to confirm. Moment assumes wall simply supported at top and bottom for M = wh²/8. Verify boundary conditions for your project. Self-weight of wall is NOT automatically added to P. Include it as part of the input P if required at the critical section. TMS 402-22 Table 8.2.5.2 Fₜ values are for in-plane running bond, normal to bed joints (bending about horizontal axis as occurs here). This calculator checks out-of-plane bending only. In-plane shear and axial load to be checked separately per TMS 402-22 Chapter 8. For walls with large openings, pilasters, or non-uniform loading, additional analysis is required beyond this uniform-wall check. ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub×
